statement: For the first bounded implementation of a new subsystem, prefer one compact module with clear internal stages over over-splitting into many files; split later when a second or third real use case appears.
notes: Captured from the first PEHA naming resolver build: one resolver file was faster and safer than premature modularization.

statement: New support layers such as caches, memory artifacts, or provider seams should be optional and additive at first; when absent, the main code path must still behave like the pre-change implementation.
notes: Seen in naming scout cache and provider adapter work: optional layers reduce rollout risk and preserve existing behavior.

statement: Wrap concrete providers behind a local adapter seam before adding more providers; the contract matters more than the current vendor.
notes: Useful both for API integrations and for temporary manual-to-API transitions.

statement: When replacing an existing result path, first compute the new result alongside the old one, compare the outputs, and only then switch by a narrow allowlist or override path.
notes: This keeps changes bounded and makes regressions visible early.

statement: Executable contracts and structured config should live in code or config files, while future ideas belong in backlog documents and live execution state belongs in state artifacts; avoid spawning new architectural prose docs for every implementation step.
notes: Helps keep implementation guidance discoverable and reduces documentation sprawl.

statement: For bounded changes, add targeted tests for the new branch conditions first, then run the relevant full suite to confirm there was no regression outside the local patch.
notes: This matched how cache, naming rules, and export integration were stabilized.

statement: When a compatibility layer needs multiple exported status flags, keep one canonical enum as the only writable source of truth and derive the helper flags in one place; compatibility cleanup should narrow edge cases around the legacy wrapper instead of adding parallel truth fields.
notes: Captured from the photo-status contract rollout: photo_status is canonical, helper fields are derived, and remaining work stays bounded to legacy wrapper cleanup plus final cutover.

statement: After a policy cutover, compatibility fields that must remain in exported bundles should mirror the authoritative decision record directly instead of being recomputed through a second legacy rules path.
notes: Captured from the bundle.card_status cutover: top-level card_status and string review reasons now mirror policy_decision_v2, while the legacy wrapper remains only as a helper for bounded compatibility tests/callers.

statement: Within the local R1 workspace, canonical raw product photos should live in downloads/photos, while scout_cache stores structured scout artifacts and scout_assets remains scratch for non-canonical exploratory outputs; scratch paths must not silently become canonical product-photo references.
notes: Captured from the manual photo-scout hygiene patch that preserved 033588.17 on the existing canonical cached photo instead of promoting a weaker scout candidate.

statement: When a manual scout path reuses deterministic trust logic across legacy and vNext registries, normalize missing source roles from the stronger known trust tier instead of silently under-classifying a trusted domain as organic discovery.
notes: Captured from price_manual_scout: Modern Eastern was trusted as industrial in legacy trust but missing from the vNext role registry, so the manual runner now derives industrial_distributor instead of leaving the source role as organic_discovery.

statement: For exact-product distributor pages, a locale or mirror host change is a real source-surface change, not a cosmetic equivalent; when source-surface stability is already established on one admissible distributor surface, manual seeds should preserve that canonical surface unless a bounded review intentionally approves the replacement.
notes: Captured from 1006186: at.rs-online.com looked exact and trustworthy, but the prior stabilized admissible surface was twen.rs-online.com, so switching hosts triggered a real surface conflict until the seed aligned to the canonical RS surface.

statement: When currency normalization is optional or incomplete, emit an explicit normalization status and reason code instead of leaving a silent null in the normalized amount field.
notes: Captured from the manual price robustness batch: TWD stayed acceptable as a source currency, but rub_price now carries fx_normalization_status=fx_gap and fx_gap_reason_code=fx_rate_unavailable_for_currency instead of looking like a silent data loss.

statement: When adding a local enhancement layer for product photos, keep raw assets as the only canonical inputs and materialize enhanced outputs as separate derivative files with deterministic naming plus explicit raw-to-derivative lineage metadata.
notes: Captured from the first photo_enhance_local rollout: downloads/photos stays canonical, downloads/photos_enhanced stores derived placeholders, and manifests tie the derivative back to the raw source SHA.

statement: Local batch runners that consume JSONL seeds in a Windows-heavy workflow should tolerate utf-8-sig input instead of assuming BOM-free utf-8, so routine PowerShell-generated seed files do not fail at load time.
notes: Captured from the target20 photo_enhance expansion: the first PowerShell-authored seed carried a UTF-8 BOM and the runner needed utf-8-sig reading to keep the bounded batch path operational.

statement: When a generation layer already has an upstream manual or model verdict cache for asset quality, the generator should honor that cache as a hard preflight gate instead of recreating outputs for known rejected sources.
notes: Captured from photo_enhance_local: downloads/photo_verdict.json now blocks known bad raw photos before enhancement and surfaces old generated files as stale cleanup candidates rather than silently preserving them as valid outputs.

statement: When refreshing legacy evidence bundles with new local content or merchandising layers, prefer a conservative overlay on a known-good evidence base over full evidence re-materialization; only the fields directly targeted by the bounded refresh should change, while stronger legacy evidence should remain intact unless a specific override is intentional.
notes: Captured from the local catalog refresh batch: full rebuild on old evidence triggered stricter identity guards and collapsed export coverage, while overlay refresh preserved the working slice and allowed targeted photo-verdict downgrades plus seeded content updates.
